title: Filter Not Detected or Not Installed
shortDescription: The purifier cannot detect an installed filter or the filter is missing.
fullDescription: Error F2 on Dyson air purifiers indicates the HEPA and/or activated carbon filter is not detected. The filter may not be properly seated, the filter housing is not closed, or the RFID chip on the filter cannot be read.

causes.cause: Filter not properly seated in the housing
Filter housing cover not fully closed
RFID chip on filter damaged or dirty

symptoms: F2 displayed on the LCD screen
Purifier refuses to start
Filter icon flashes on display
Message in MyDyson app about filter

fixSteps.title: Reseat the filter
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off and unplug the purifier. Open the filter housing, remove the filter(s), and reinstall them firmly ensuring they click into place.

fixSteps.title: Check the filter housing
fixSteps.instruction: Ensure the housing cover is fully closed and latched. Some models have magnetic closures that must align properly.

fixSteps.title: Clean the RFID contact area
fixSteps.instruction: Wipe the RFID area on the filter and inside the housing with a dry cloth. Dust buildup can interfere with detection.

fixSteps.title: Try a new filter
fixSteps.instruction: If the error persists with properly seated filters, the filter RFID may be damaged. Try a new genuine Dyson filter.

whenToCallPro: If the RFID reader on the machine has failed.
videoSearchQuery: Dyson air purifier F2 filter not detected fix

faq.q: Does Dyson require genuine filters?
faq.a: Dyson purifiers use RFID chips to verify filters. Non-genuine filters without RFID will trigger F2.

faq.q: How often should I replace Dyson purifier filters?
faq.a: Dyson recommends replacement every 12 months, though this varies by usage and air quality.

faq.q: Can I reset the filter counter without replacing?
faq.a: The filter counter should only be reset after installing a new filter. Resetting with an old filter defeats the purpose.

title: Filter Life Expired
shortDescription: The filter has reached its maximum usage life and needs replacement.
fullDescription: F3 indicates the filter has been in use beyond its recommended lifespan. The purifier tracks filter usage through airflow hours and alerts when replacement is due. The machine may continue to run but filtration effectiveness is compromised.

causes.cause: Filter has been in use for 12+ months
Filter used in high-pollution environment degrading faster
Filter counter not reset after previous replacement

symptoms: F3 on the LCD display
Filter life shows 0% in MyDyson app
Notification in the app to replace filter
Reduced air purification performance

fixSteps.title: Order replacement filters
fixSteps.instruction: Purchase genuine Dyson replacement filters for your specific model. Different models require different filter types.

fixSteps.title: Replace the filters
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off and unplug the purifier. Open the filter housing, remove old filters, and install new ones following the arrows for correct orientation.

fixSteps.title: Reset the filter counter
fixSteps.instruction: In the MyDyson app, go to the device settings and select Reset Filter Life. On some models, hold the oscillation button for 5 seconds while powered on.

fixSteps.title: Verify the reset
fixSteps.instruction: Check the app or LCD display to confirm the filter life shows 100% or the F3 error has cleared.

whenToCallPro: Not needed. Filter replacement is always DIY.
videoSearchQuery: Dyson air purifier F3 filter replacement reset

faq.q: Can I keep using the purifier with F3?
faq.a: The purifier will continue to run, but filtration effectiveness is significantly reduced.

faq.q: How do I reset the filter counter on Dyson?
faq.a: Use the MyDyson app or hold the oscillation button for 5 seconds on most models.

faq.q: Are HEPA and carbon filters replaced separately?
faq.a: On some models they are separate, on others they are integrated. Check your model's requirements.

title: Airflow Restriction Detected
shortDescription: The purifier has detected restricted airflow through the filters or air channels.
fullDescription: F9 indicates that airflow through the purifier is significantly reduced. This is typically caused by heavily clogged filters, a blocked air intake, or obstructions in the amplifier loop.

causes.cause: Air intake vents blocked by objects or dust

symptoms: F9 on the LCD display
Fan sounds strained or louder than normal
Reduced airflow from the outlet
Purifier cycles on and off

fixSteps.title: Check air intake vents
fixSteps.instruction: Ensure nothing is blocking the air intake openings on the purifier body. Move the unit away from walls (at least 20cm clearance).

fixSteps.title: Clean or replace filters
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the filters and inspect them. If heavily discolored, replace them. Light dust can be addressed by gentle vacuuming of the filter surface.

fixSteps.title: Clear the amplifier loop
fixSteps.instruction: Check the air outlet loop or blade for obstructions. Wipe the inside of the loop with a soft cloth.

fixSteps.title: Restart the purifier
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug for 30 seconds and restart. The F9 error should clear once airflow is restored.

whenToCallPro: If airflow is restricted even with new filters and clear vents.
videoSearchQuery: Dyson air purifier F9 airflow restriction fix

faq.q: How much clearance does a Dyson purifier need?
faq.a: At least 20cm (8 inches) from walls on all sides for proper air intake.

faq.q: Can I vacuum the Dyson filter?
faq.a: You can gently vacuum the outside surface to remove surface dust, but this does not replace proper filter replacement.

faq.q: Why does my Dyson purifier keep turning off?
faq.a: F9 airflow restrictions can cause the motor to overheat and cycle off. Clear the airflow path.

title: Air Quality Sensor Malfunction
shortDescription: The particulate matter or VOC sensor is not functioning correctly.
fullDescription: E2 indicates that one of the air quality sensors (PM2.5, PM10, VOC, or NO2) has failed or is reporting abnormal readings. The purifier may still run but Auto mode cannot adjust based on air quality.

causes.cause: Sensor lens dirty from accumulated dust
Sensor exposed to chemicals or strong VOCs

symptoms: E2 on the display
Air quality readings show dashes or zero
Auto mode does not adjust fan speed
Sensor data missing from MyDyson app

fixSteps.title: Clean the sensor opening
fixSteps.instruction: Locate the sensor inlet (small opening on the body). Use a dry cotton swab to gently clean inside the sensor opening. Use compressed air to blow out dust.

fixSteps.title: Restart the purifier
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug the purifier for 60 seconds to allow the sensor to recalibrate when powered back on.

fixSteps.title: Allow calibration time
fixSteps.instruction: After cleaning, the sensor may need 30-60 minutes to recalibrate. Leave the purifier running in a well-ventilated area.

fixSteps.title: Avoid chemical exposure
fixSteps.instruction: Do not use aerosol sprays, strong cleaning chemicals, or incense near the sensor opening, as these can temporarily overwhelm or damage the sensor.

whenToCallPro: If the sensor does not recover after cleaning and recalibration.
videoSearchQuery: Dyson air purifier E2 sensor error fix cleaning

faq.q: What sensors does a Dyson purifier have?
faq.a: Most models have PM2.5, PM10 particle sensors, a VOC/formaldehyde sensor, and a humidity/temperature sensor.

faq.q: Can aerosol sprays damage the sensor?
faq.a: Yes, strong chemicals and aerosols can overwhelm or damage the VOC sensor. Avoid using them near the purifier.

faq.q: Does the purifier still clean air without the sensor?
faq.a: Yes, but Auto mode will not function properly. You can manually set the fan speed.

title: Wi-Fi or Communication Error
shortDescription: The purifier cannot connect to Wi-Fi or has lost communication with the MyDyson app.
fullDescription: E3 indicates a Wi-Fi connectivity issue. The purifier cannot connect to the home network or has lost the connection to Dyson cloud services.

causes.cause: Wi-Fi password changed or network down
Purifier too far from the router

symptoms: E3 on the display
Cannot control purifier from app
No remote data or scheduling
Wi-Fi icon absent from display

fixSteps.title: Check your Wi-Fi network
fixSteps.instruction: Verify your home Wi-Fi is working and other devices can connect. Dyson purifiers only support 2.4GHz networks.

fixSteps.title: Move closer to router
fixSteps.instruction: If the purifier is far from the router, move it closer or add a Wi-Fi extender.

fixSteps.title: Reconnect to Wi-Fi
fixSteps.instruction: In the MyDyson app, go to the device settings and select reconnect to Wi-Fi. Follow the setup process.

fixSteps.title: Factory reset the Wi-Fi
fixSteps.instruction: Hold the power button for 20 seconds to reset the Wi-Fi module. Then re-pair using the MyDyson app.

whenToCallPro: Only if the Wi-Fi module has failed hardware-level.
videoSearchQuery: Dyson air purifier E3 Wi-Fi connection error fix

faq.q: Does Dyson purifier work without Wi-Fi?
faq.a: Yes, all functions work with the remote control. Wi-Fi adds app control, scheduling, and air quality monitoring.

faq.q: Why does Dyson need 2.4GHz Wi-Fi?
faq.a: The Wi-Fi module in Dyson purifiers only supports the 2.4GHz band. Make sure your router has this enabled separately from 5GHz.

faq.q: How do I factory reset the Wi-Fi?
faq.a: Hold the power button for 20 seconds. The display will show the reset confirmation.

title: Motor Fault
shortDescription: The fan motor has encountered an error and cannot operate normally.
fullDescription: E4 indicates a motor fault. The fan motor may be stalled, overheating, or experiencing an electrical issue. The motor protection circuit has engaged.

causes.cause: Motor overheated from continuous max operation
Foreign object in the fan area

symptoms: E4 on the display
Fan does not spin or spins erratically
Unusual noise from the motor area
Purifier cuts out during operation

fixSteps.title: Let the unit cool down
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug the purifier and let it sit for 30-60 minutes to cool down completely.

fixSteps.title: Check for foreign objects
fixSteps.instruction: Look into the air outlet and intake for any objects that could obstruct the fan.

fixSteps.title: Restart after cooling
fixSteps.instruction: Plug back in and turn on. If the motor starts normally, it was a thermal protection event.

fixSteps.title: Contact Dyson support
fixSteps.instruction: If E4 persists after cooling, the motor likely has a hardware fault requiring professional repair.

whenToCallPro: If the error persists after cooling and checking for obstructions.
videoSearchQuery: Dyson air purifier E4 motor fault fix

faq.q: Can running on max speed damage the motor?
faq.a: Running on max continuously for extended periods can cause thermal protection to activate. Use Auto mode for normal operation.

faq.q: Is E4 covered under warranty?
faq.a: Motor faults within the warranty period are typically covered. Check your warranty status.

faq.q: Can I replace the motor myself?
faq.a: Motor replacement requires significant disassembly and is best done by Dyson service technicians.

title: Heater Element Fault (Hot+Cool Models)
shortDescription: The heating element is not functioning properly on Hot+Cool models.
fullDescription: H1 applies to Dyson Purifier Hot+Cool models and indicates the heating element or its control circuitry has a fault. The purifier will not produce warm air but may still function as a fan and purifier.
symptoms: H1 on the display
No warm air produced in heat mode
Heat mode icon flashes but air stays cool
Fan and purifier functions work normally

fixSteps.title: Reset the purifier
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug the unit for 60 seconds and restart. This may reset a tripped thermal fuse.

fixSteps.title: Check for obstructions
fixSteps.instruction: Ensure nothing is blocking the air outlet or is placed too close to the unit, which could cause overheating and thermal cutout.

fixSteps.title: Verify the room temperature
fixSteps.instruction: If the room is already at or above the set temperature, the heater may not activate. Set a higher target temperature.

fixSteps.title: Contact Dyson support
fixSteps.instruction: Heater element repairs require professional service. Contact Dyson for warranty or repair options.

whenToCallPro: If the heater does not work after a power reset.
videoSearchQuery: Dyson Hot+Cool heater not working H1 error fix

faq.q: Can I still use my Dyson as a fan if the heater is broken?
faq.a: Yes, the fan and air purification functions are independent of the heater and should work normally.

faq.q: Why did my heater stop working?
faq.a: The thermal fuse may have tripped from overheating, or the heater element has reached end of life.

faq.q: Is the heater repair expensive?
faq.a: Heater element replacement typically costs $100-250 including parts and labor.

title: Formaldehyde Sensor Calibration Required
shortDescription: The formaldehyde detection sensor needs recalibration or attention.
fullDescription: On Dyson models with the solid-state formaldehyde sensor (Formaldehyde variants), this alert indicates the HCHO sensor is not reading correctly and may need recalibration or the catalytic filter needs attention.

causes.cause: Sensor exposed to high concentrations of chemicals

symptoms: HCHO reading shows unusual values
Formaldehyde reading stuck at zero or very high
Alert in MyDyson app about sensor
Auto mode over-reacts or under-reacts to formaldehyde

fixSteps.title: Run recalibration
fixSteps.instruction: In the MyDyson app, go to device settings and run the formaldehyde sensor recalibration process. Keep the unit running in a clean-air environment for 2-4 hours.

fixSteps.title: Ventilate the room
fixSteps.instruction: Open windows and ventilate the room for 30 minutes before recalibrating. High background VOC levels can interfere.

fixSteps.title: Check catalytic filter
fixSteps.instruction: The formaldehyde variants have a catalytic oxidation filter that does not need replacement but should be clean. Gently vacuum its surface.

fixSteps.title: Update firmware
fixSteps.instruction: Ensure the purifier has the latest firmware for improved sensor algorithms.

affectedModels: Dyson Purifier Big Quiet Formaldehyde BP04
whenToCallPro: If the sensor does not calibrate after multiple attempts.
videoSearchQuery: Dyson Formaldehyde sensor calibration HCHO fix

faq.q: Does the formaldehyde filter need replacement?
faq.a: No. The catalytic oxidation filter is designed to last the lifetime of the machine and continuously destroys formaldehyde.

faq.q: How accurate is the HCHO sensor?
faq.a: Dyson's solid-state sensor is accurate to 0.01 mg/m3 and more reliable than gel-based sensors in other purifiers.

faq.q: What produces formaldehyde in my home?
faq.a: New furniture, paints, cleaning products, pressed wood products, and some fabrics can off-gas formaldehyde.
